questions.q: What does const {a, b} = {a: 1, b: 2, c: 3} assign?
questions.explanation: Object destructuring extracts named properties. a and b are pulled from the object with their values 1 and 2. Property c is ignored. Option 1 would occur if a and b don't exist in the source object.
The spread operator ... unpacks array elements inline. [...[1, 2], 3] spreads [1, 2] to 1, 2 and appends 3, yielding [1, 2, 3]. Option 1 treats the array as a single nested element.

questions.q: What is the output of const f = (x) => x * 2; f(3)?
questions.explanation: Arrow function (x) => x * 2 implicitly returns x * 2 for single expressions. f(3) evaluates to 3 * 2 = 6. Option 1 returns the expression string, not the computed result.
Template literals (backticks) evaluate expressions inside ${}. 5 + 3 = 8, interpolated as 'hello 8'. Option 1 treats the template as literal text. Option 2 ignores the template evaluation.

questions.q: What does const f = (a, b = 10) => a + b; f(5) return?
questions.explanation: Default parameters fill in when arguments are omitted. f(5) sets a=5, b=10 (default), returning 5 + 10 = 15. Option 1 treats the missing b as 0 or NaN.

questions.q: What does (a, b) => a > b ? 'yes' : 'no' do with (3, 5)?
questions.explanation: The ternary operator condition ? true_val : false_val tests 3 > 5 (false), returning 'no'. Option 1 returns the opposite. Option 2 returns the raw boolean.
Destructuring with defaults: x=10 from the object, y=20 from the default. Missing properties use their defaults. Option 1 ignores the default value.

questions.q: What does function foo(a, ...rest) allow rest to capture?
options: All remaining positional arguments as an array
questions.explanation: Rest parameters ...rest collect excess positional arguments into an array. foo(1, 2, 3, 4) sets a=1, rest=[2, 3, 4]. Option 1 treats rest as a single value. Option 2 requires destructuring syntax.
Array destructuring with holes skips elements. The empty slot skips index 1 (value 2). Result: a=1, c=3. Option 1 assigns c the skipped value.

questions.q: What does ({a, b} = {a: 1, b: 2}) => a + b return when called with no args?
questions.explanation: Default parameters in destructuring: if no argument, use {a: 1, b: 2}. a=1, b=2, a+b=3. Option 1 treats missing arguments as undefined in the destructure.
